judge_reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content (a newspaper clipping with a sketch of a flying saucer, a 10FT vertical arrow, a 20FT horizontal dimension, and three labeled smaller views), but Description B adds specific details about the newspaper's date, location, headline, and artist credit that Description A does not mention, without contradicting Description A's visual claims.
original_image_tags: A torn, aged, yellowed newspaper clipping from the "Times" newspaper. The paper is dark brown/orange with age, with ragged edges, particularly on the left and top. The top left corner of the newspaper name is torn off.

A black-and-white artist's sketch of a flying saucer. The main illustration shows a wide, flat, disc-shaped object with a central dome and a thick, cylindrical raised section around the dome. It is emitting fiery plumes from its side. To the left is a vertical arrow labeled "10FT" indicating height. Below the main drawing are three smaller view diagrams labeled "SIDE VIEW" (showing a low profile), "BOTTOM VIEW" (showing a circle with a central point), and "END VIEW" (showing a triangular profile). A horizontal dimension line across the main disc is labeled "20 FT".
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: A scanned newspaper front page with a large black-and-white illustration of a flying saucer or disc, labeled “SIDE VIEW,” “BOTTOM VIEW,” and “END VIEW,” with hand-drawn dimension markings “10 ft” and “20 ft.”*
